title: Why Are You Writing Such Despondent Letters?
usage_rules: Prefer Wikimedia Commons categories and file pages with explicit license/public-domain information.
For modern icons/photos, keep attribution and license in asset_attribution.json.
Do not assume every icon image is free just because the saint is old; verify the file page license.
Use icons reverently: no distorted faces, no mocking zooms, no sensational effects.
Mix icon/portrait assets with monastery exteriors, manuscripts, maps, candles, and landscape footage to avoid a static slideshow.

scenes.title: A Hard Mercy
scenes.visual_prompt: Cinematic Orthodox letter on desk, Nikon portrait, dim candlelight, a spiritual daughter reading in silence, no melodrama.

beats.narration_excerpt: The question sounds almost severe: why are you writing such despondent letters?  A softer teacher might have answered only with sympathy.
beats.asset_note: Scene anchor: use the saint portrait/icon as a reverent recurring visual.
beats.search_query: Orthodox saint icon portrait Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: Hegumen Nikon Vorobiev answered with something harder, and perhaps more merciful.  He saw despondency not only as pain, but as a spiritual danger that could swa...
beats.asset_note: Use manuscripts, handwritten letters, old books, or generated letter-table closeups.
beats.search_query: hard mercy hegumen nikon vorobiev answered something Russian manuscript letter book Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: He was not mocking the wounded soul.  He was refusing to let despair become the final word.
beats.asset_note: Use manuscripts, handwritten letters, old books, or generated letter-table closeups.
beats.search_query: hard mercy mocking wounded soul refusing despair Russian manuscript letter book Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: In a letter to a spiritual daughter, he speaks like an elder who knows suffering from the inside, and who still insists that the soul must turn toward God.

scenes.title: Do Not Trust the Dark Thought
scenes.visual_prompt: A handwritten page half in shadow and half in candlelight, Jesus Prayer rope, quiet spiritual warfare without horror imagery.

beats.narration_excerpt: One of the hidden dangers of despondency is that it speaks with false authority.  The dark thought says: this is the truth about your life, this is the truth ab...

beats.search_query: Orthodox saint icon portrait Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: A thought can feel powerful and still be false.  A mood can feel total and still be temporary.  The Christian does not have to obey every interior accusation.
beats.asset_note: Use the saint portrait/icon as a reverent anchor image.
beats.search_query: Orthodox saint icon portrait Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: The soul must learn to bring thoughts into prayer, into confession, and into the light of Christ, where they can be tested.

scenes.title: Suffering Without Self-Pity

scenes.title: Refusing Despair
scenes.visual_prompt: Letter closing beside Nikon portrait and candle, dawn over Russian church, quiet hope after darkness.

beats.narration_excerpt: We can grieve, repent, ask for help, and still turn toward Christ.  We can be weak and still pray.  We can fall and still return.
